note: Implemented entries are runnable from this repository. Planned entries are public benchmarks that require optional datasets, services, or heavier dependencies.

benchmarks.name: Agent user-memory retrieval
benchmarks.dataset: 200 synthetic user facts, 50 natural-language Russian queries
benchmarks.target: Match Chroma precision@1 on static recall, beat it on precision@3, and keep avg latency below 5 ms at 200 memories.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the same benchmark with sentence-transformers and a FAISS-backed candidate index.

benchmarks.name: Agent coherence and token savings
benchmarks.dataset: 500-memory long user history with corrections, TTL, namespace isolation, project recall, and repeated personalization tasks
benchmarks.target: Show that dynamic memory improves agent behavior, not only retrieval: higher task success, fewer stale errors, longer coherent runs, and compact context.
benchmarks.next_step: Move this scenario from deterministic task scoring to LLM answer-quality scoring on LoCoMo/LongMemEval-style tasks.

benchmarks.name: Dynamic memory policy
benchmarks.dataset: Hot memory, TTL, correction, and namespace checks over 200 memories
benchmarks.target: Keep precision@1 and stale suppression at 1.00 while reducing avg latency below 10 ms at 1000 memories.
benchmarks.next_step: Add Chroma metadata-policy and Qdrant payload-filter baselines so the comparison is not only static-vector search.

benchmarks.name: Field memory graph dynamics
benchmarks.dataset: 13 deterministic memories: conflicting facts, related concept memories, activation spreading, inhibition, and decay
benchmarks.target: Keep graph precision@1, stale suppression, concept formation, and concept consolidation at 1.00 while moving the same memory dynamics into LoCoMo/LongMemEval evidence tasks.
benchmarks.next_step: Make MemoryFieldGraph incremental and evaluate conflict/update behavior on public long-memory datasets.

benchmarks.name: WaveMind capacity curve
benchmarks.dataset: Static and dynamic agent-memory checks at 200, 1000, and 5000 memories
benchmarks.target: Hold precision@1 >= 0.95 at 5000 memories and avg dynamic query latency below 20 ms.
benchmarks.next_step: Move candidate generation to FAISS/Annoy and limit wave-field reranking to the top candidate set.

benchmarks.name: Long-term memory evidence
benchmarks.dataset: Synthetic long-memory evidence scenario with profile, preference, correction, TTL, namespace, and filler history
benchmarks.target: Keep this as a small regression check for stale suppression, correction, namespace isolation, and personalization.
benchmarks.next_step: Use public LoCoMo and LongMemEval for external claims; keep this synthetic scenario for fast regression testing.

benchmarks.name: BEIR-style open retrieval runner
benchmarks.dataset: Any local BEIR-style corpus.jsonl, queries.jsonl, and qrels/<split>.tsv dataset.
benchmarks.target: Run WaveMind, Chroma, and Qdrant with identical embeddings and compare retrieval/index behavior on public qrels.
benchmarks.next_step: Add sentence-transformers runs for SciFact, then add NFCorpus as the second BEIR dataset.

benchmarks.name: NoMIRACL Russian retrieval
benchmarks.dataset: NoMIRACL Russian test relevant subset, 200 queries, 5000 compact top-k candidate passages, human-annotated relevance labels.
benchmarks.target: Reach same-embedding Russian nDCG@10 parity with Chroma/Qdrant while moving latency below 5 ms through FAISS/service-mode candidate indexes.
benchmarks.next_step: Run sentence-transformers on the same NoMIRACL Russian split and then add the full MIRACL Russian corpus profile when disk/service capacity allows it.

benchmarks.name: LoCoMo evidence retrieval runner
benchmarks.dataset: Official LoCoMo locomo10.json, using conversation turns as memories and QA evidence dialog ids as relevance labels.
benchmarks.target: Improve LoCoMo evidence_recall@5 with semantic embeddings and keep retrieval latency below 20 ms for WaveMind.
benchmarks.next_step: Add LoCoMo answer generation with a local LLM and measure answer accuracy/faithfulness.

benchmarks.name: LongMemEval evidence retrieval
benchmarks.dataset: Official LongMemEval-S cleaned file, 470 non-abstention questions, 22419 session memories, session-level evidence retrieval.
benchmarks.target: Keep full LongMemEval-S evidence recall above static vector-store baselines while staying below 20 ms retrieval latency.
benchmarks.next_step: Run turn-level evidence mode and sentence-transformers, then add LLM answer accuracy/abstention evaluation.

benchmarks.name: LongMemEval evidence 50-query smoke
benchmarks.dataset: Official LongMemEval-S cleaned file, first 50 non-abstention questions, session-level evidence retrieval.
benchmarks.target: Keep this smoke profile above Chroma/Qdrant evidence recall while using it as the fast regression check before full LongMemEval reruns.
benchmarks.next_step: Speed up full LongMemEval reruns by reusing per-question candidate indexes or adding a streaming runner mode.

benchmarks.name: ANN index latency curve
benchmarks.dataset: Generated normalized 128-d vectors at 1000, 5000, 10000, and 50000 points; recall@10 measured against exact cosine neighbors.
benchmarks.target: At 50000 vectors, keep recall@10 above 0.95 while reducing latency below exact NumPy or move this role to a production vector index.
benchmarks.next_step: Tune quantized search kernels, add FAISS on Linux/macOS CI, and test Qdrant service-mode curves beyond 50000 vectors.

benchmarks.name: Production index profile
benchmarks.dataset: Docker-backed 50000-vector profile comparing persisted FAISS, Qdrant service, and PostgreSQL/pgvector HNSW.
benchmarks.target: Keep persisted FAISS and service-mode vector backends at recall@10 >= 0.95 while staying below 10 ms average query latency at 50000 vectors.
benchmarks.next_step: Use the dedicated production load profile for 100000 and 1000000-vector service tests, then tune pgvector and Qdrant for recall/latency.

benchmarks.name: Production pgvector tuning profile
benchmarks.dataset: Docker-backed PostgreSQL/pgvector tuning profile over 10000 and 50000 generated normalized 128-d vectors, with Qdrant service as the reference service baseline.
benchmarks.target: Use pgvector exact as the recall floor and keep pgvector iterative above recall@10 0.95 with p99 below 100 ms at 50000 vectors.
benchmarks.next_step: Promote pgvector iterative from the 50000-vector tuning profile into the 100k/1M production load SLO profiles when disk and build time allow it.

benchmarks.name: Production load profile 100k
benchmarks.dataset: 100000 generated normalized 128-d vectors; recall@10 measured against exact cosine neighbors.

WaveMind faiss-persisted.reason: Set WAVEMIND_FAISS_PATH to use the persisted FAISS backend

benchmarks.target: Reach recall@10 >= 0.95 and p99 latency < 100 ms on at least one production service backend at 100000 memories.
benchmarks.next_step: Keep at least one service backend at SLO pass while adding persisted FAISS from the Linux benchmark container and raising the target QPS.

benchmarks.name: Production load profile 1M
benchmarks.dataset: 1000000 generated normalized 128-d vectors; FAISS persisted and Qdrant service recall@10/latency profiles.
benchmarks.target: Keep recall@10 >= 0.95 and push p99 latency below 100 ms at 1M vectors.
benchmarks.next_step: Keep the FAISS persisted 1M profile green and tune Qdrant/pgvector so service-mode vector DB backends also pass recall and p99.

benchmarks.name: Qdrant 1M HNSW ef sweep
benchmarks.dataset: 1000000 generated normalized 128-d vectors; one Qdrant service collection queried with multiple hnsw_ef settings.
benchmarks.target: Find a setting that keeps recall@10 >= 0.95 while keeping p99 latency below 100 ms.
benchmarks.next_step: Repeat with 100+ queries and collection-level HNSW build parameters; the current best recall setting still misses the p99 SLO.

benchmarks.name: Production streaming load runner
benchmarks.dataset: Memory-bounded streaming generator for 10M and 50M target-recall load profiles. Checked-in artifacts include 10k smoke plus 100k, 1M, and 10M compressed FAISS IVF-PQ profiles, plus a 50M plan-only resource/command preflight.
benchmarks.target: Keep 10M compressed FAISS IVF-PQ above recall@10 0.95 and p99 below 100 ms, keep the 50M preflight reproducible, then run 50M and add Qdrant/pgvector service backends on hardware sized for the index.
benchmarks.next_step: Set WAVEMIND_FAISS_IVFPQ_PATH and run the checked 50M reproduction command, then add service-backed Qdrant/pgvector 10M artifacts for broader production evidence.

benchmarks.name: Scale readiness profile
benchmarks.dataset: Deterministic 1M-memory simulation for namespace placement, cluster autoscale planning, Kubernetes StatefulSet/CronJob/HPA generation, Knative/KEDA serverless plan generation, quorum runtime, service-mode replica repair, real HTTP shard transport, service-mode tombstone repair, anti-entropy repair worker, cursor-based active-active delta sync, field-only hotness delta sync, field-state CRDT convergence, replicated snapshot/offsite/archive restore, S3-compatible object-store upload verification, query-audit cache prewarm, query-vector cache, Redis-compatible shared rate limiting, Memory OS adaptive prewarm/consolidation/forgetting plus production architecture advice, hot-cache, API cache mutation safety, and structured-payload retrieval checks.
benchmarks.target: Prove the production foundation before heavier 100k, 1M, 10M, and 100M vector load tests: deterministic placement, cluster autoscale planning, Kubernetes deployment, HPA autoscaling, serverless scale-to-zero planning, scheduled repair manifests, service-mode distributed namespace sharding, real HTTP shard transport, sustained mixed HTTP cluster load, missing-replica repair, tombstone-aware delete repair, anti-entropy repair worker, survivable replicas, cursor-based active-active sync, field-only hotness sync, field-state convergence, offsite/archive/object-store upload/latest-metadata/download/retention/DR-drill checks, query-vector cache, shared rate limiting, Memory OS adaptive prewarm/consolidation/forgetting with production architecture advice, hot-cache behavior, API cache mutation safety, structured payload recall, and a 100M-memory capacity envelope.
benchmarks.next_step: Move from deterministic 100M capacity planning to service-backed 100M Qdrant/pgvector/FAISS load tests on sized hardware.

benchmarks.name: Production readiness gate
benchmarks.dataset: Gate generated from checked-in benchmark artifacts: production load SLO/cost, cluster placement, Kubernetes/operator output, serverless state externalization, cache/prewarm, query-vector cache, shared rate limiting, API cache mutation safety, Memory OS adaptive worker and embedded production architecture advice, distributed repair, cursor-based active-active CRDT convergence, backups, structured payloads, and 10M-load presence. External competitor-service evidence is tracked separately.
benchmarks.target: Reach readiness_score 1.0 with zero action_required items before claiming complete million-plus production readiness.
benchmarks.next_step: Keep the gate at readiness_score 1.0 while repeating larger service-backed runs and moving external competitor evidence into the separate adapter profile.

benchmarks.name: Local HTTP cluster smoke
benchmarks.dataset: Four real localhost WaveMind API processes with isolated SQLite stores. The workload checks quorum writes, normal queries, simulated node failover queries, missing-replica repair, replicated forget, delete suppression, p99, and SLO status.
benchmarks.target: Keep success_rate at 1.0, failover_hit_rate at 1.0, delete_suppression_rate at 1.0, and p99 below 1000 ms in CI before promoting remote service-node deployments.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the same workload against external service nodes and then increase namespace count and payload size on sized hardware.

benchmarks.name: External HTTP cluster load runner
benchmarks.dataset: Real WaveMind HTTP API-node sustained workload: quorum writes, normal queries, simulated node failover queries, missing-replica repair, replicated forget, delete suppression, and SLO verdict over user-supplied node URLs.
benchmarks.target: Keep the service-node workload green against real API processes, then repeat it against remote Kubernetes or serverless nodes before claiming external-cluster production readiness.
benchmarks.next_step: Replace the current loopback service-node artifact with a remote node manifest run from a multi-node deployment.

benchmarks.name: Memory competitor adapter profile
benchmarks.dataset: Small dynamic-memory adapter profile covering correction, TTL, namespace isolation, and preferences.

Zep.reason: Set ZEP_API_URL or ZEP_API_KEY to run the Zep adapter profile.

GraphRAG static graph.backend: local lexical entity graph over active facts

benchmarks.target: Keep Mem0, LangGraph, and GraphRAG-style local adapter results checked in, then check in a live Zep Cloud or OSS-compatible service run once ZEP_API_URL or ZEP_API_KEY is configured.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the live Zep adapter against a configured service and expand the GraphRAG baseline from this small static graph to a larger update/conflict workload.

benchmarks.name: LongMemEval answer generation
benchmarks.dataset: LongMemEval-S questions answered from compact retrieved evidence. Checked-in local runs compare WaveMind, Chroma, and Qdrant using Ollama qwen2.5:0.5b and qwen2.5:1.5b over the first 50 non-abstention questions.
benchmarks.target: Move from lightweight local smoke runs to full LongMemEval-S answer generation with stronger local and API models, then compare against Chroma/Qdrant RAG.
benchmarks.next_step: Run all 470 non-abstention questions with a stronger local/API model and add LLM-judge faithfulness scoring.

benchmarks.name: BEIR
benchmarks.dataset: Heterogeneous zero-shot information retrieval benchmark across many public datasets.
benchmarks.target: On identical embeddings, stay within 0.02 nDCG@10 of Chroma/Qdrant and keep WaveMind reranking latency below 10 ms.
benchmarks.next_step: Implement a BEIR adapter for SciFact and NFCorpus first because they are small enough for local CI smoke runs.

benchmarks.name: MTEB Retrieval
benchmarks.dataset: Massive Text Embedding Benchmark retrieval tasks across many datasets and languages.
benchmarks.target: Use MTEB to separate encoder quality from memory-policy quality; WaveMind should not reduce same-embedding retrieval quality.
benchmarks.next_step: Run a small retrieval subset with hash, sentence-transformers, and a modern multilingual embedding model.

benchmarks.name: MIRACL Russian
benchmarks.dataset: Native-speaker judged multilingual retrieval benchmark with Russian included. NoMIRACL Russian compact candidate benchmark is implemented; full-corpus MIRACL remains planned.
benchmarks.target: Prove Russian recall with semantic embeddings; target nDCG@10 parity with same-embedding Chroma/Qdrant.
benchmarks.next_step: Extend the NoMIRACL loader to full MIRACL Russian corpus once disk/service capacity is available.

benchmarks.name: VectorDBBench
benchmarks.dataset: VectorDBBench custom dataset export with train/test/neighbors/scalar-label parquet files. Checked-in manifest points to a reproducible 10000-vector, 100-query, 128-d cosine dataset.
benchmarks.target: Use official VectorDBBench runs to compare WaveMind's production index paths without pretending NumPy local mode is a cloud vector database.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the generated custom dataset through official VectorDBBench targets for Qdrant, Milvus, pgvector, and WaveMind-backed FAISS/Qdrant profiles.

benchmarks.name: LoCoMo answer generation
benchmarks.dataset: LoCoMo questions answered from retrieved evidence with an LLM.
benchmarks.target: Beat static vector-store RAG on temporal/correction questions by at least 15 percentage points while returning compact evidence.
benchmarks.next_step: After the retrieval-only LoCoMo run is published, add an optional Ollama answer-generation layer using the user's installed local model.

benchmarks.name: LongMemEval-V2
benchmarks.dataset: Web-agent histories with state recall, dynamic state tracking, workflow knowledge, gotchas, and premise awareness.
benchmarks.target: Become a compact evidence retriever for agent trajectories, with explicit wins on dynamic state tracking and gotcha recall.
benchmarks.next_step: Prototype trajectory-to-memory segmentation and compare compact WaveMind evidence against plain chunk retrieval.

benchmarks.name: LMEB
benchmarks.dataset: Long-horizon Memory Embedding Benchmark across episodic, dialogue, semantic, and procedural memory tasks.
benchmarks.target: Use LMEB to choose the default semantic encoder and prove that memory retrieval is not just passage retrieval.
benchmarks.next_step: Run LMEB retrieval tasks once optional dataset downloads are available outside restricted CI.

benchmarks.name: RAGBench
benchmarks.dataset: Large RAG evaluation dataset with industry-style corpora and explainable labels.
benchmarks.target: Show whether WaveMind's dynamic suppression improves context quality when facts become stale or conflicting.
benchmarks.next_step: Add an optional RAGBench adapter after retrieval benchmarks are stable.
